description: §39.3 compile-time db-anchor rejection — a `<schema>` block is placed correctly as an immediate child of `<program>`, but the `<program>` root has no `db=` attribute. §39.1/§39.3: a `<schema>` reads its database path from the enclosing `<program db="...">`, so a `<schema>` without that anchor is a compile error (E-SCHEMA-001). The gauntlet declaration check (checkSchemaDeclarations) walks the AST, pairs each `<schema>` node with its nearest enclosing `<program>` root, and rejects the block when that root carries no `db=`.
rationale: §39.12 error table + §39.3 normative statement: a `<schema>` block SHALL be valid only inside a file whose `<program>` root element has a `db=` attribute. A `<schema>` without an enclosing `<program db="...">` SHALL be a compile error (E-SCHEMA-001). Resolution: add a `db=` attribute to the `<program>` root (e.g. `<program db="./app.db">`). The standalone no-`<program>` case is instead reported as E-SCHEMA-003 (placement).
